Every current owner rejects fixed pass/correction ritual while preserving Effect boundary and helper-admission rules and requiring boundary-matched review.
Docs-writing guidance is clean-clone portable and routes to current repository owners without ritual.

impactLedger.acceptance: Semantic classifiers reject fixed coordination counts and generic SDK callbacks, raw string IDs, primitive config, instanceof policy, and unchecked SDK output; the accepted fixtures and canonical skill code pass.

verification.result: 12 tests passed, including accepted/rejected semantic coordination and provider-boundary fixtures plus a scan of every current HGI-201 owner.

Repository path/type accounting for 541 text and eight binary paths, Effect language-service, lint, format, 12 skill tests, Knip, production Knip, and 23 workspace typecheck/build tasks passed after the evaluator-driven correction.

The first evaluator correctly rejected fixed three-pass ritual in two omitted current owners and phrase-only contradiction coverage. Both owners were corrected and the policy test now traverses every current owner and accepted/rejected provider code fixtures.

An independent worker reproduced quick validation and 12 skill-policy tests, confirmed all ten current owners reject ritual, verified the provider fixtures catch all five prohibited patterns while accepted examples pass, and found no remaining acceptance blocker.

limitations: A scoped Git receipt and remote readback remain required before HGI-201 closes.
Local checks do not prove release, publication, registry, provider, deployment, production, or external consumer state.
nonClaims: No provider, external planning workspace, release, publication, or deployment was accessed.
No target-owned runbook or verification route was created.
rollback: Revert this bounded HGI-201 candidate slice; leave HGI-200 and retained historical/public evidence untouched.
nextTrigger: Commit and publish the accepted semantic slice, record exact Git identity/readback, then proceed with HGI-207.
